seoTitle: Fitzgibbon vs Fitzroy Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fitzgibbon and Fitzroy Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fitzgibbon vs Fitzroy Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fitzgibbon and Fitzroy Island.
heading: Fitzgibbon vs Fitzroy Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fitzgibbon, Queensland and Fitzroy Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fitzgibbon has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Fitzroy Island (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fitzgibbon or Fitzroy Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fitzgibbon has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Fitzroy Island scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fitzgibbon scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Fitzroy Island scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fitzgibbon or Fitzroy Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fitzgibbon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
